tool.name: rdeval report
tool.command: #import re mkdir render && cd render && export RDEVAL_SHARE_DIR="\$(dirname \$(dirname \$(type -P rdeval)))/share/rdeval" && ln -s "\$RDEVAL_SHARE_DIR/figures.Rmd" && ln -s "\$RDEVAL_SHARE_DIR/rdeval_interface.R" && #for $input_file in $input_files #set $safe_name = re.sub(r"[^\w\-.]", "_", str($input_file.element_identifier)) ln -s '$input_file' '${safe_name}.rd' && #end for Rscript '$render_script'

configfiles.configfile: ## R script to render figures #import re #set $input_file_list = [] #for $input_file in $input_files #set $safe_name = re.sub(r"[^\w\-.]", "_", str($input_file.element_identifier)) #silent $input_file_list.append(f"'{safe_name}.rd'") #end for #set $r_vector = "c(" + ",".join($input_file_list) + ")" #set $interactive_value = "FALSE" #if $interactive #set $interactive_value = "TRUE" #end if rmarkdown::render( 'figures.Rmd', output_file='$outfile', output_format='html_document', params=list( input_files=$r_vector, interactive='$interactive_value' ) )

tool.help: This tool creates a report containing a read summary and figures from input RD files.
